name: bubblewrap
title: bubblewrap
summary: utility for unprivileged chroot and namespace manipulation
description:
utility for unprivileged chroot and namespace manipulation
bubblewrap uses Linux namespaces to launch unprivileged containers.
These containers can be used to sandbox semi-trusted applications such
as Flatpak apps, image/video thumbnailers and web browser components,
or to run programs in a different library stack such as a Flatpak runtime
or a different Debian release.
.
This package relies on a kernel with user namespaces enabled.
Official Debian and Ubuntu kernels are suitable.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use bwrap because utility for unprivileged chroot and namespace manipulation.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use bwrap as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bwrap [OPTION...] [COMMAND]` as documented upstream for bwrap.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair bwrap with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `bwrap --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install bubblewrap. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `bwrap [OPTION...] [COMMAND]`. utility for unprivileged chroot and namespace manipulation.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: utility for unprivileged chroot and namespace manipulation.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: utility for unprivileged chroot and namespace manipulation.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install bubblewrap
Install with: sudo apt install bubblewrap
